issues.description:
## Input

Signal from 2026-06-14 Workspace Upgrade Report (DEV-260):
- 19 blocked issues, 9 of which are blocked specifically waiting on admin credentials:
  - DEV-108: GH PAT for newtechkw org + codegraph install + Pulse v3 approval (escalation_deadline=2026-06-14 TODAY, waiting_on present since 2026-06-07)
  - DEV-93: GitHub PAT (RW + RO) for Build Squad agents
  - DEV-96: CONTEXT7_API_KEY for code agents
  - DEV-97: Sentry credentials for deployment/QA/security agents
  - DEV-126: FIGMA_TOKEN for figma-to-ux-spec skill
  - DEV-169: CEO desktop daemon offline (urgent, since 2026-05-31)
  - DEV-197: ADR-002 App Hub Model blockers (re-escalation, since 2026-06-01)
  - DEV-39 + DEV-40: adminnewtech/attendance-app GitHub repo not provisioned
- No workspace memory entry exists that standardises: (a) which credentials are outstanding, (b) when each was first requested, (c) re-escalation cadence (current pattern: silent drift for weeks).

## Expected Output

A set of memory entries (one per outstanding admin blocker) that the CEO/autopilot can load on each run to:
1. Know which credentials are still missing without re-scanning all blocked issues.
2. Know when each was first requested and when to re-escalate.
3. Know the escalation contact (admin@newtechkw.com) and the issue to comment on.

Concrete deliverables:
- Create/update workspace memory entries documenting the outstanding blockers using available memory tooling (note in comment if a `multica memory` command exists; otherwise document the default fact-capture approach used in CLAUDE.md).
- If no multica memory CLI exists, post a structured `##Admin Blockers Summary` comment on the Workspace Operations Dialog (DEV-36 is excluded per rules — find an appropriate ops issue or post to the parent of DEV-108) so the pattern survives across agent runs.
- Recommended memory keys per blocker: `credential_name`, `first_requested_at`, `escalation_deadline`, `waiting_on_issue`, `re_escalation_interval_days`.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All 9 admin-blocked issues captured with: credential name, first-request date, deadline/urgency
- [ ] Re-escalation cadence defined: suggest every 7 days for medium-priority, every 3 days for urgent (DEV-169)
- [ ] Memory accessible to future CEO runs (either workspace memory or pinned ops comment)
- [ ] DEV-108 escalation_deadline=2026-06-14 flagged as overdue → post a re-escalation note on DEV-108
- [ ] No secrets or credential values stored — only the names/types of missing credentials

## Dependencies

- DEV-108 (parent blocker, today's deadline): issue ID `4850d6c3-e432-4b72-9add-1d0e27a7cfe5`
- DEV-169 (urgent runtime offline): issue ID `4ba6e4f2-a285-4048-add8-389d8a5f5dbc`
- No external blockers for capture; admin must supply credentials to actually unblock

## Risk

Low. Read-only scan + memory write. No side effects on issues except adding a re-escalation comment on DEV-108 (which is appropriate and explicitly needed — deadline passed today).

## Definition of Done

- Memory entries or structured comment created for all 9 admin blockers
- Re-escalation note posted on DEV-108 (overdue today)
- DEV-169 urgent runtime blocker flagged as top priority for admin
- Result comment on this issue listing what was captured
issues.title: Workspace Upgrade Action — Capture admin-escalation blockers as memory defaults

issues.description:
Daily Command Center Digest. Mode=create_issue. Runs every day 07:00 Asia/Kuwait (GMT+3). Produces ONE per-project status briefing comment on the dated issue this autopilot creates, so the CEO + workspace owner can see — at a glance — every project's state and what to push today.

CRITICAL SAFETY RULES:
- Mode is create_issue. Do not create additional issues. Do not change status on any other issue.
- NEVER mention any agent or member in the digest comment. Owner reads the dated issue directly; mentions would loop (Factory Pulse no-mention rule).
- NEVER touch DEV-36 (workspace-operations dialog) — exclude it from every list, count, and section.
- Quiet on green days: if NOTHING actionable changed since yesterday's digest (no new blockers, no stale in_review, no new approval ask, no project lifecycle_phase change, no new app-request), do NOT post any comment. Leave the auto-created issue body empty so it can be auto-cancelled by step (8).
- Use `--output json` on every CLI call. Continue on single-call failure (log to stdout and skip that signal).

STEPS:

(1) Snapshot the workspace.
    - `multica project list --output json` → all projects.
    - `multica issue list --output json` → all issues (paged if needed).
    - For each non-cancelled issue: record id, identifier, title, status, project_id, parent_issue_id, assignee_id, assignee_type, updated_at.
    - Exclude DEV-36 (id e60d5be5-b9dd-48aa-b949-28286ea80f1b) everywhere.
    - Build agent + squad name maps once: `multica agent list --output json` and `multica squad list --output json`. Used for assignee names; never for mention links.

(2) Detect "since yesterday" deltas. Treat `since = (now - 24h)` UTC.
    - new_blockers: issues that flipped to status=blocked in the window (updated_at >= since AND status=blocked).
    - cleared_blockers: issues that left blocked in the window (status != blocked AND prior blocked_reason metadata key present — best-effort).
    - new_approval_asks: any issue whose metadata gained `waiting_on=owner` or `waiting_on=admin` in the window, or whose body/comments contain a fresh "Approval needed" line written in the window (cheap heuristic: latest comment created_at >= since and contains "approval needed" case-insensitive).
    - stale_in_review_48h: any issue status=in_review with updated_at older than 48h.
    - lifecycle_phase changes: any parent issue whose metadata `lifecycle_phase` changed (compare to value from yesterday's pin — see step 7).
    - new_app_requests: any issue created_at >= since in project `01 - App Requests` (f3089a97-c608-4599-913c-f331335e1abe).
    If ALL six lists are empty AND there is no stale in_review and no current blocked anywhere → green day. Skip to step (8) without posting.

(3) Per-project section assembly. For each project (sorted by project title prefix `00 -`, `01 -`, … and then APP projects):
    - Skip the project entirely if it has zero open issues (status in {todo, in_progress, in_review, blocked}) AND no new_app_requests AND no lifecycle change.
    - Goal: 1 sentence from project.description (truncate to ~140 chars at the first sentence boundary).
    - lifecycle_phase: read from the project's "anchor" parent issue (heuristic: the parent issue with the most children in this project; if APP project, the parent whose title starts with "APP — "). Show the value of metadata `lifecycle_phase` or "—" if unset.
    - Active issues: counts by status — `todo=N in_progress=N in_review=N blocked=N` (exclude DEV-36).
    - Blockers + waiting_on: list every issue in this project with status=blocked OR metadata `waiting_on` set. Format: `DEV-NN <title> — waiting_on=<value> | blocked_reason=<value>`. Cap 5 per project; "+N more" if truncated.
    - Next action + owner: the single highest-priority issue in this project that is `todo` or `in_progress` and not blocked. Format: `DEV-NN <title> — owner=<agent-or-squad name>` (plain name, NO mention link).
    - Risk: 1 line — pick the most material risk: stale in_review item, oldest blocker, or "WIP imbalance: <name> has N in_progress" if any agent in this project has >=4 open issues. If nothing material, write "Risk: low — no stale or overloaded items.".
    - Approval needed: yes/no. If yes, quote the exact ask (one sentence from the requesting issue's body/comment). Format: `Approval needed: yes — DEV-NN: "<exact ask>"` or `Approval needed: no`.

(4) Compose the Top-3 block. Score every open issue across the workspace by:
    - urgency: blocked=4, stale_in_review_48h=3, has approval ask=3, deploy_gate=red=4, priority=urgent=4 / high=3 / medium=2 / low=1.
    - momentum: in_progress=2, in_review=1, todo=1, backlog=0.
    - age penalty: +1 per 24h since updated_at, cap +3.
    Final score = urgency + momentum + age_penalty. Pick the top 3 distinct issues (no two from the same parent unless nothing else qualifies).

(5) Render the digest comment using this exact shape (parseable by humans and future audits):

```
# Daily Command Center Digest — <YYYY-MM-DD KW>

**Window:** <since> → <now> (UTC) | **Projects scanned:** N | **Open issues:** N | **Blocked:** N | **Stale in_review (48h):** N | **Approval asks:** N

## Per-Project Status

### <project icon> <project title>
- **Goal:** <one sentence>
- **Phase:** <lifecycle_phase>
- **Active:** todo=<n> in_progress=<n> in_review=<n> blocked=<n>
- **Blockers / waiting_on:** <list or "none">
- **Next action:** <DEV-NN title — owner=name>
- **Risk:** <one line>
- **Approval needed:** <yes/no> [— "<exact ask>"]

### <next project>
… (same shape)

## Top 3 to Push Today

1. **DEV-NN** <title> — assignee=<name> | why: <one phrase>
2. **DEV-NN** <title> — assignee=<name> | why: <one phrase>
3. **DEV-NN** <title> — assignee=<name> | why: <one phrase>

## Deltas Since Yesterday
- New blockers: <DEV-NN list or "none">
- Cleared blockers: <DEV-NN list or "none">
- New approval asks: <DEV-NN list or "none">
- Stale in_review (48h+): <DEV-NN list or "none">
- New app requests: <DEV-NN list or "none">
- Lifecycle phase changes: <project: old→new or "none">
```

Post via: `multica issue comment add <this-issue-id> --content-stdin` (HEREDOC the body). NEVER include any `mention://agent/...` or `mention://member/...` link. Issue references should be plain `DEV-NN` text (no `mention://issue/...` either — keep the digest scannable).

(6) On a green day (step 2 short-circuited), do NOT post any comment. Skip to step (8).

(7) Pin metadata on THIS issue only if a value materially helps tomorrow's run:
    - `last_run_verdict` = "green" or "posted" (overwrite each run).
    - `last_window_end` = <RFC3339-now> (overwrite each run).
    - `lifecycle_phase_snapshot` = JSON map `{project_id: phase}` so step (2) can detect phase changes tomorrow.
    - `top3_count` = number (expect 0 on green days, 3 otherwise).
    Use `multica issue metadata set` once per changed key. Skip any key whose value is identical to what `metadata list` already shows.

(8) Status finalization.
    - If a digest was posted: flip THIS issue to `in_review` (`multica issue status <this-issue-id> in_review`). CEO + owner read it, then close.
    - If green day (no digest posted): flip THIS issue to `cancelled` with no comment so it stops cluttering the inbox.

(9) Output one line to stdout (only non-comment output): `Daily Digest @ <RFC3339> | verdict=<green|posted> | projects=N | top3=N | blocked=B stale=S approvals=A`.

OPERATING RULES:
- Never block on a single CLI failure. Log "skipped <signal>: <reason>" and continue with the remaining signals.
- All comments go ONLY on the issue this autopilot creates. Never comment on any other issue.
- Never mention any agent or member anywhere. Plain names only.
- Read this issue's existing metadata before pinning. Only update keys whose values changed.
- Do not create sub-issues, do not reassign any other issue, do not change any other issue's status.
- The created issue MUST land in `in_review` (when posted) or `cancelled` (green day) — never `done` (owner closes), never left in `todo`.

*Autopilot run triggered at 2026-06-20 07:00 Asia/Kuwait. After starting work, rename this issue to accurately reflect what you are doing.*
issues.title: Daily Command Center Digest — 2026-06-20

issues.description:
Run the daily factory health audit. Cover: (1) runtime health (multica runtime list) — alert if any offline >5m; (2) WIP discipline — flag any agent with >3 in_progress or any squad with >6; (3) stale issues — list any in_progress issue >24h with no comments; (4) orphan/zero-skill check — every skill assigned to ≥1 agent; every agent has ≥1 skill; (5) metadata hygiene — sample 10 done issues and flag any with stale waiting_on / blocked_reason; (6) cost — pull the current month spend per project and compare to budget (cost-budget-tracker); (7) error budget — pull yesterday's Sentry error rate, latency p95, uptime % per shipped app and alert on SLO burn. Post one consolidated comment on the created issue with: GREEN/YELLOW/RED status, the top 5 issues found, and one-line remediation per issue. Status the issue 'done' if all green; 'in_review' if YELLOW; 'blocked' if RED. Do not silently ignore failures.

issues.description:
# Skill: weekly-status-email

## Purpose

Generate a structured weekly status email summarizing what the team shipped, current blockers, and priorities for the coming week. Designed to be invoked by any team member or agent at the end of each work week or sprint, requiring no manual formatting.

## Trigger

Invoke when a team member or manager asks for a weekly status update email (e.g. "send the weekly update", "draft the status email for this week", "write the end-of-sprint email"). Also suitable for automation on a recurring Friday/end-of-sprint schedule.

## Required Input

- **project** — The project name or ID to query for closed issues (e.g. `DEV`, `88c09466-8a25-419d-b28e-d3d5973197c4`).
- **week_start** — ISO date for the start of the reporting period (e.g. `2026-06-09`).
- **week_end** — ISO date for the end of the reporting period (e.g. `2026-06-15`).
- **recipient** — (Optional) Name of the recipient or audience. Defaults to "Team" if not provided.

## Steps

1. Run `multica issue list --project <project> --status done --output json` (or equivalent) to retrieve all issues closed during the `week_start`–`week_end` period.
2. Filter to issues whose `updated_at` or `done_at` falls within the date range.
3. Extract human-readable titles only — strip internal IDs, URLs, and technical identifiers from the output.
4. Query for any issues currently in `blocked` status within the same project to populate the Blockers section.
5. Query for issues in `todo` or `in_progress` status to determine next week's priorities (top 5 by priority field).
6. Compose the email:
   - **Subject:** `Weekly Status Update — Week of <week_start>`
   - **Greeting:** `Hi <recipient>,`
   - **What we shipped:** Bullet list of closed issue titles (plain language, no IDs).
   - **Blockers:** Bullet list of blocked issue titles + one-line blocker reason if available in the issue description. If none, write "No active blockers."
   - **Next week:** Top 5 priority issue titles from `todo`/`in_progress`.
   - **Sign-off:** `Best, [Agent / Team]`
7. Output the email as plain text (no markdown headers, no internal URLs, no issue identifiers).
8. Present the draft to the invoker for approval before sending (if a send action is available).

## Output

A plain-text email body ready to send or paste:
- Subject line
- Greeting
- "What we shipped" section (bullet list, human-readable titles only)
- "Blockers" section (bullet list or "No active blockers")
- "Next week" section (up to 5 priorities)
- Sign-off line

Maximum length: 400 words. Tone: professional, concise, readable by non-technical stakeholders.

## Quality Checklist

- [ ] All four required inputs are present or defaulted before running.
- [ ] Closed issues are filtered to the exact `week_start`–`week_end` window.
- [ ] No internal issue IDs, UUIDs, or URLs appear in the email body.
- [ ] Blockers section is present (even if it says "No active blockers").
- [ ] Next week section lists ≤ 5 items.
- [ ] Email is plain text with no markdown headers or code blocks.
- [ ] Draft is presented for human approval before any send action.

## Failure Handling

- **No closed issues found for the period:** Write "No issues were closed this week." in the shipped section and note it may mean the date range is wrong — ask the invoker to confirm.
- **Project not found or access denied:** Report the error, ask the invoker to confirm the project name/ID, and retry.
- **Blocker query fails:** Omit the blockers section and note it in the email draft as "Blocker data unavailable — check manually."
- **Draft exceeds 400 words:** Summarize each bullet to ≤ 15 words and truncate to top 7 shipped items.

## Improvement Rule

Review after 5 uses. Refine if:
- Recipients report the email is too long or technical.
- Closed-issue filtering misses or double-counts items (adjust date filter logic).
- A scheduling autopilot is set up — add a step to post the draft to a Slack channel automatically.
- Multica adds a native reporting export (retire the manual query steps).

issues.description:
# ADR-003 — Mobile Layer Default (Expo + EAS Build)

**Status:** Adopted  
**Date:** 2026-06-01  
**Deciders:** Solution Architect (solution-architect), CEO Command Squad  
**Extends:** ADR-001 (DEV-128) — adds the mobile layer omitted from the web-stack ADR  
**Supersedes:** —  
**Superseded by:** —

---

## Context

ADR-001 (DEV-128) established the default web stack (Next.js + Supabase + Vercel) but left the mobile layer undefined. The Android Attendance app (DEV-43, DEV-30) was built using Expo + local Gradle without a formal ADR backing that decision. The `android-apk-local-builder` workspace skill has accumulated `retry_log=3` QA gate failures (DEV-174), signalling that local APK builds are fragile in the current environment. Future mobile apps (HR mobile, delivery driver app) need a consistent, documented baseline to avoid stack divergence.

---

## Decision

The devosnt App Factory adopts **Expo (React Native)** as its default mobile framework with the following build split:

| Environment | Build system | Output |
|---|---|---|
| Development / local | `eas build --local` (Gradle) | Debug APK |
| Staging / preview | EAS Build cloud (free tier) | Signed internal APK |
| Production / release | EAS Build cloud | Signed release AAB / APK |

This decision covers Android-first mobile apps. iOS coverage via EAS Build is provisioned when the client supplies an Apple Developer account.

> **Interim rule (until DEV-174 is `done`):** the `eas build --local` path is considered unstable. All builds, including development, must use EAS Build cloud until the android-apk-local-builder skill is validated.

---

## Layer-by-Layer Rationale

### Framework — Expo (React Native)
- React Native shares ~90% of code with the web React codebase; the App Factory's agents already know React.
- Expo managed workflow eliminates native boilerplate: camera, notifications, location, and file system are SDK modules requiring no Xcode or Android Studio configuration.
- Expo SDK is compatible with the Supabase JS client — the same auth library used in the web stack.
- TypeScript end-to-end consistency with Next.js frontend; shared types across layers are feasible.
- Expo SDK ships quarterly releases with documented upgrade tooling (`npx expo upgrade`).

### Build — EAS Build (Expo cloud CI) for staging and production
- Managed signing: Android keystores stored in Expo's secure vault; no local keystore management.
- Reproducible build environment: Docker-based, pinned to exact Expo SDK + Node + Gradle versions.
- OTA updates via Expo Updates: JS-layer fixes ship without a new APK Play Store review cycle.
- EAS Submit automates Play Store upload.
- Free tier: 30 builds/month; production apps move to EAS Production plan.

### Build — `eas build --local` for development (when DEV-174 is resolved)
- Zero cloud cost and no queue wait during fast iteration.
- Requires: pinned `JAVA_HOME`, `GRADLE_VERSION` in `eas.json`, and a Docker-based local environment.
- Gated on DEV-174 fix; not used in any new app until the skill passes its QA gate.

---

## When to Deviate

### Deviate to native Android (Kotlin) when ANY of the following apply:
1. **AR/VR or computer vision** requiring custom OpenGL/Vulkan pipelines or camera below 30 fps.
2. **Bluetooth LE / NFC POS integrations** with no Expo SDK module and no viable community module.
3. **Heavy background processing** (continuous GPS at <1 s interval, audio streaming) that Expo background tasks cannot support.
4. **Security hardware modules** (HSM, Secure Enclave beyond standard biometrics).
5. **Client mandate**: client explicitly requires a native Kotlin codebase with in-house mobile engineers.

### Deviate to Web PWA (Next.js) instead of a native app when:
- App is informational / read-heavy with occasional form input.
- No native device APIs required (no camera, no push notifications, no offline-first data).
- Time-to-market ≤ 2 weeks and the client cannot manage app store distribution.

All deviations require a written ADR amendment approved by the Solution Architect and CEO Command Squad **before** any build work starts.

---

## Minimum Viable Mobile Build Checklist

Every Expo app must pass this checklist before the QA gate (`in_review` → `done`):

### Environment variables
- [ ] `EXPO_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL` — Supabase project URL
- [ ] `EXPO_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY` — Supabase anon/public key
- [ ] `EXPO_PUBLIC_API_BASE_URL` — set only if API is not Supabase direct
- [ ] Secrets (service role key, payment keys) stored in **EAS Secrets vault**, never in `.env` or `app.config.js`

### `app.config.js` / `app.json`
- [ ] `expo.android.package` set to `com.newtechkw.<appname>` (reverse domain)
- [ ] `expo.version` and `expo.android.versionCode` bumped for every release build
- [ ] `expo.plugins` lists all native Expo SDK modules used

### `eas.json`
- [ ] `development` profile: `"buildType": "apk"`, `"distribution": "internal"`
- [ ] `preview` profile: `"buildType": "apk"`, `"distribution": "internal"`, `"env"` block for staging Supabase URL
- [ ] `production` profile: `"buildType": "app-bundle"`, `"distribution": "store"`, `"autoIncrement": true`

### Signing (production only)
- [ ] Android keystore generated and stored via `eas credentials` (EAS vault)
- [ ] SHA-1 / SHA-256 fingerprints documented in the build issue metadata (`signing_fingerprint`)
- [ ] Keystore backed up to client's 1Password vault or equivalent

### QA gate items (mobile-specific)
- [ ] Build succeeds via `eas build --profile preview --platform android`
- [ ] Auth flow tested on physical device or emulator API 33+
- [ ] OTA update channel configured for production (`"channel": "production"` in `eas.json`)
- [ ] No hardcoded URLs or secrets in JS bundle (grep check: `grep -rE "(supabase|_key|secret|password)" src/`)
- [ ] App cold-start < 3 s on mid-range Android (2 GB RAM)

---

## Reversal Conditions

This ADR is reconsidered if **any** hold:

1. Expo SDK drops React Native support or licence changes post-acquisition.
2. EAS Build cloud cost exceeds $500/month workspace-wide (evaluate self-hosted Turtle CLI).
3. More than 50% of new app requests require features on the deviation list above.
4. React Native's New Architecture (Fabric + JSI) causes breaking regressions across 2+ consecutive Expo SDK upgrades.

---

## Validation Path

- **DEV-174** (android-apk-local-builder skill fix) is the active validation task. This ADR's `eas build --local` path is gated on DEV-174 reaching `done`.
- When DEV-174 closes, the Solution Architect must update the **Interim rule** section above to remove the restriction.

---

## References

- Expo SDK docs: https://docs.expo.dev
- EAS Build: https://docs.expo.dev/build/introduction/
- EAS Credentials (managed signing): https://docs.expo.dev/app-signing/managed-credentials/
- React Native New Architecture: https://reactnative.dev/docs/the-new-architecture/landing-page
- DEV-43: Android Attendance build — Expo + Gradle precedent
- DEV-30: Architecture: Android Attendance — first Expo usage
- DEV-128: ADR-001 — Default Tech Stack (this ADR extends it)
- DEV-174: android-apk-local-builder skill fix (validation path, gated)

## Input

Signal: 33 of 38 in_review issues are stale >48h (87%). The pattern has recurred across at least 3 consecutive weeks (confirmed in DEV-171, last week's Upgrade Report, and this week's DEV-211). Root cause: CEO quality-gate sweep dispatches QA Gate sub-issues but the in_review queue is never actively triaged or batch-closed. Autopilot-generated Daily Digest and Factory Health Audit issues accumulate with no cleanup cadence. No workspace memory captures this pattern for future CEO runs.

Score: 20 (Impact=4, Effort=S, Confidence=5)
Source: Workspace Upgrade Report 2026-06-07, DEV-211

## Expected Output

Two new memory entries in the workspace memory system:

1. **Pattern memory:** "In_review queue clogs weekly — CEO must proactively triage stale autopilot digest/audit issues, not just dispatch QA Gate sub-issues. As of 2026-06-07: 33 of 38 in_review issues are stale >48h. Autopilot-generated issues (Daily Digest, Factory Health Audit, Workspace Upgrade Report) with last_run_verdict=posted and no open children can be marked done without QA gate."

2. **Standing rule memory:** "Every CEO run: after dispatching QA Gate sub-issues, also sweep for autopilot digest/audit issues in in_review stale >7 days and close them directly (mark done). These are completed reports, not shippable work — they do not need a QA gate."

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Read the existing workspace memory index and confirm no duplicate entry exists
- [ ] Write memory entry 1 (pattern observation with date and count)
- [ ] Write memory entry 2 (standing CEO rule for digest/audit cleanup)
- [ ] Update the memory index (MEMORY.md) with both new entries
- [ ] Post a result comment on DEV-211 with the memory slugs and their locations

## Dependencies
- Access to workspace memory directory (already exists)
- Understanding of the CEO agent prompt and in_review sweep logic

## Risk
Low — memory entries are additive and do not change any code or agent prompts. They provide context that the CEO agent reads at the start of future runs.

## Definition of Done
- Two memory files written with correct frontmatter (type: feedback + type: project)
- MEMORY.md index updated
- Result comment posted on DEV-211

issues.description:
Daily Command Center Digest. Mode=create_issue. Runs every day 07:00 Asia/Kuwait (GMT+3). Produces ONE per-project status briefing comment on the dated issue this autopilot creates, so the CEO + workspace owner can see — at a glance — every project's state and what to push today.

CRITICAL SAFETY RULES:
- Mode is create_issue. Do not create additional issues. Do not change status on any other issue.
- NEVER mention any agent or member in the digest comment. Owner reads the dated issue directly; mentions would loop (Factory Pulse no-mention rule).
- NEVER touch DEV-36 (workspace-operations dialog) — exclude it from every list, count, and section.
- Quiet on green days: if NOTHING actionable changed since yesterday's digest (no new blockers, no stale in_review, no new approval ask, no project lifecycle_phase change, no new app-request), do NOT post any comment. Leave the auto-created issue body empty so it can be auto-cancelled by step (8).
- Use `--output json` on every CLI call. Continue on single-call failure (log to stdout and skip that signal).

STEPS:

(1) Snapshot the workspace.
    - `multica project list --output json` → all projects.
    - `multica issue list --output json` → all issues (paged if needed).
    - For each non-cancelled issue: record id, identifier, title, status, project_id, parent_issue_id, assignee_id, assignee_type, updated_at.
    - Exclude DEV-36 (id e60d5be5-b9dd-48aa-b949-28286ea80f1b) everywhere.
    - Build agent + squad name maps once: `multica agent list --output json` and `multica squad list --output json`. Used for assignee names; never for mention links.

(2) Detect "since yesterday" deltas. Treat `since = (now - 24h)` UTC.
    - new_blockers: issues that flipped to status=blocked in the window (updated_at >= since AND status=blocked).
    - cleared_blockers: issues that left blocked in the window (status != blocked AND prior blocked_reason metadata key present — best-effort).
    - new_approval_asks: any issue whose metadata gained `waiting_on=owner` or `waiting_on=admin` in the window, or whose body/comments contain a fresh "Approval needed" line written in the window (cheap heuristic: latest comment created_at >= since and contains "approval needed" case-insensitive).
    - stale_in_review_48h: any issue status=in_review with updated_at older than 48h.
    - lifecycle_phase changes: any parent issue whose metadata `lifecycle_phase` changed (compare to value from yesterday's pin — see step 7).
    - new_app_requests: any issue created_at >= since in project `01 - App Requests` (f3089a97-c608-4599-913c-f331335e1abe).
    If ALL six lists are empty AND there is no stale in_review and no current blocked anywhere → green day. Skip to step (8) without posting.

(3) Per-project section assembly. For each project (sorted by project title prefix `00 -`, `01 -`, … and then APP projects):
    - Skip the project entirely if it has zero open issues (status in {todo, in_progress, in_review, blocked}) AND no new_app_requests AND no lifecycle change.
    - Goal: 1 sentence from project.description (truncate to ~140 chars at the first sentence boundary).
    - lifecycle_phase: read from the project's "anchor" parent issue (heuristic: the parent issue with the most children in this project; if APP project, the parent whose title starts with "APP — "). Show the value of metadata `lifecycle_phase` or "—" if unset.
    - Active issues: counts by status — `todo=N in_progress=N in_review=N blocked=N` (exclude DEV-36).
    - Blockers + waiting_on: list every issue in this project with status=blocked OR metadata `waiting_on` set. Format: `DEV-NN <title> — waiting_on=<value> | blocked_reason=<value>`. Cap 5 per project; "+N more" if truncated.
    - Next action + owner: the single highest-priority issue in this project that is `todo` or `in_progress` and not blocked. Format: `DEV-NN <title> — owner=<agent-or-squad name>` (plain name, NO mention link).
    - Risk: 1 line — pick the most material risk: stale in_review item, oldest blocker, or "WIP imbalance: <name> has N in_progress" if any agent in this project has >=4 open issues. If nothing material, write "Risk: low — no stale or overloaded items.".
    - Approval needed: yes/no. If yes, quote the exact ask (one sentence from the requesting issue's body/comment). Format: `Approval needed: yes — DEV-NN: "<exact ask>"` or `Approval needed: no`.

(4) Compose the Top-3 block. Score every open issue across the workspace by:
    - urgency: blocked=4, stale_in_review_48h=3, has approval ask=3, deploy_gate=red=4, priority=urgent=4 / high=3 / medium=2 / low=1.
    - momentum: in_progress=2, in_review=1, todo=1, backlog=0.
    - age penalty: +1 per 24h since updated_at, cap +3.
    Final score = urgency + momentum + age_penalty. Pick the top 3 distinct issues (no two from the same parent unless nothing else qualifies).

(5) Render the digest comment using this exact shape (parseable by humans and future audits):

```
# Daily Command Center Digest — <YYYY-MM-DD KW>

**Window:** <since> → <now> (UTC) | **Projects scanned:** N | **Open issues:** N | **Blocked:** N | **Stale in_review (48h):** N | **Approval asks:** N

## Per-Project Status

### <project icon> <project title>
- **Goal:** <one sentence>
- **Phase:** <lifecycle_phase>
- **Active:** todo=<n> in_progress=<n> in_review=<n> blocked=<n>
- **Blockers / waiting_on:** <list or "none">
- **Next action:** <DEV-NN title — owner=name>
- **Risk:** <one line>
- **Approval needed:** <yes/no> [— "<exact ask>"]

### <next project>
… (same shape)

## Top 3 to Push Today

1. **DEV-NN** <title> — assignee=<name> | why: <one phrase>
2. **DEV-NN** <title> — assignee=<name> | why: <one phrase>
3. **DEV-NN** <title> — assignee=<name> | why: <one phrase>

## Deltas Since Yesterday
- New blockers: <DEV-NN list or "none">
- Cleared blockers: <DEV-NN list or "none">
- New approval asks: <DEV-NN list or "none">
- Stale in_review (48h+): <DEV-NN list or "none">
- New app requests: <DEV-NN list or "none">
- Lifecycle phase changes: <project: old→new or "none">
```

Post via: `multica issue comment add <this-issue-id> --content-stdin` (HEREDOC the body). NEVER include any `mention://agent/...` or `mention://member/...` link. Issue references should be plain `DEV-NN` text (no `mention://issue/...` either — keep the digest scannable).

(6) On a green day (step 2 short-circuited), do NOT post any comment. Skip to step (8).

(7) Pin metadata on THIS issue only if a value materially helps tomorrow's run:
    - `last_run_verdict` = "green" or "posted" (overwrite each run).
    - `last_window_end` = <RFC3339-now> (overwrite each run).
    - `lifecycle_phase_snapshot` = JSON map `{project_id: phase}` so step (2) can detect phase changes tomorrow.
    - `top3_count` = number (expect 0 on green days, 3 otherwise).
    Use `multica issue metadata set` once per changed key. Skip any key whose value is identical to what `metadata list` already shows.

(8) Status finalization.
    - If a digest was posted: flip THIS issue to `in_review` (`multica issue status <this-issue-id> in_review`). CEO + owner read it, then close.
    - If green day (no digest posted): flip THIS issue to `cancelled` with no comment so it stops cluttering the inbox.

(9) Output one line to stdout (only non-comment output): `Daily Digest @ <RFC3339> | verdict=<green|posted> | projects=N | top3=N | blocked=B stale=S approvals=A`.

OPERATING RULES:
- Never block on a single CLI failure. Log "skipped <signal>: <reason>" and continue with the remaining signals.
- All comments go ONLY on the issue this autopilot creates. Never comment on any other issue.
- Never mention any agent or member anywhere. Plain names only.
- Read this issue's existing metadata before pinning. Only update keys whose values changed.
- Do not create sub-issues, do not reassign any other issue, do not change any other issue's status.
- The created issue MUST land in `in_review` (when posted) or `cancelled` (green day) — never `done` (owner closes), never left in `todo`.

---

issues.description:
Weekly Benchmark Sweep — refresh the six-vendor competitive brief every Monday and route the top action into the Skill Factory. Mode=create_issue. Runs every Monday 09:00 Asia/Kuwait (GMT+3). Project: 09 - Research Intelligence (e977793d-b825-448a-a3f1-bb8f59cc1266). Source skill: `benchmark-vendor-tracker` (DEV-50). Seed data: DEV-62 (first six-vendor sweep).

CRITICAL SAFETY RULES:
- Mode is create_issue. Do not create additional issues except the up-to-3 sub-issues for Skill Factory dispatch in step (6).
- NEVER mention the CEO (d9f942b9-e245-4e28-99eb-8f12fbebc2c3) in any comment — assignment is the trigger.
- NEVER mention any agent in a wrap-up or thank-you. Only mention an agent on a fresh dispatch sub-issue, and only if assignment alone is insufficient.
- NEVER touch DEV-36 (the workspace-operations dialog) or any unrelated issue.
- Read each issue's metadata before pinning anything; do not log run state. Stay within the issue this autopilot creates and the dispatch sub-issues it spawns.
- All comments go ONLY on the issue this autopilot creates and the dispatch sub-issues spawned this run. Never comment on DEV-36, DEV-50, DEV-60, or any other issue.
- Use `--output json` on every Multica CLI call. Continue on single-call failure (log "skipped <vendor|step>: <reason>" to stdout and proceed).
- Never use curl/wget against Multica resources — only against vendor public sites/docs.

VENDORS COVERED EVERY WEEK (six, in this order):
1. Lovable           — https://lovable.dev
2. Bolt.new          — https://bolt.new
3. Cursor (agent)    — https://www.cursor.com
4. Replit Agent      — https://replit.com/agent
5. Antigravity 2.0   — https://antigravity.dev (or current official URL)
6. Atoms.dev         — https://atoms.dev

STEPS:

(1) Scope. Treat `since = now - 7 days` (RFC3339, UTC) as the diff window. Locate the prior sweep's report comment for diffing: list this project's issues created by this autopilot in the past 14 days (title starts with "Weekly Benchmark Sweep —"); pick the most recent one whose status is `done` or `in_review` and that is NOT the issue this autopilot just created. If no prior sweep is found, fall back to DEV-62 (RESEARCH: Six-vendor benchmark sweep, id c289d731-eaf9-4747-814f-c122f1ed08cd) as the baseline. If neither is available, mark the diff section "No baseline — first sweep" and proceed.

(2) Per vendor (all six, in order), invoke the `benchmark-vendor-tracker` skill to produce a fresh one-page brief. The skill's required output shape (per DEV-50) is: vendor name, run date, 3-sentence description, onboarding flow note, pricing as of run date, 3 strengths (with source links), 3 weaknesses, patterns to copy (named), patterns to beat (named, with our differentiated approach), decision verdict (import / adapt / recreate / study only / reject). If the `benchmark-vendor-tracker` skill is not yet published, use this inline fallback rubric and add a one-line note to the final report: "benchmark-vendor-tracker skill not yet published — used inline rubric." Capture every claim with a source URL — landing page, pricing page, changelog, release notes, blog post, or YouTube demo. No hand-wavy claims.

   Source-of-truth checklist per vendor (best effort; skip any that 404 or block):
   - Landing page (current copy + above-the-fold positioning)
   - Pricing page (capture tier names, prices, and any "new in <date>" badges)
   - Changelog / release notes / "What's new" page
   - Public blog or product update feed (last 30 days)
   - YouTube / X / public demo for any flow change announced in the window
   Continue on per-source failure. Cap vendor research at ~10 minutes wall time each.

(3) Diff vs last week. For each vendor, compare this week's brief to the baseline brief (from step 1). Material diff categories:
   - new_features (named, with source link)
   - pricing_changes (tier added/removed, price up/down, free-tier change)
   - new_flows (onboarding, agent UX, deployment surface, integration surface)
   - removed_or_deprecated (anything quietly dropped)
   - positioning_shift (the headline copy changed in a way that signals strategy)
   If a vendor genuinely shipped nothing material, write "No material change since <baseline date>." Do NOT pad — empty diffs are signal, not failure.

(4) Score the cross-vendor pattern set. Aggregate every "pattern to copy" across the six briefs and rank by:
   - frequency (how many of the six vendors do this)
   - impact (1–5: how much would importing this move our win rate vs Lovable/Bolt/Cursor)
   - effort (S | M | L)
   - readiness (do we already have an adjacent skill we can extend, or is this net-new)
   Final score = (frequency × impact) / effort. Pick the Top 3 "actions to consider."

(5) Compose the sweep report. Post ONE comment on this autopilot's created issue with this exact shape (parseable for the next sweep's diff step):

```
# Weekly Benchmark Sweep — <YYYY-MM-DD>

**Window:** <since> → <now> (UTC)
**Baseline brief:** DEV-NN (<date>) | **Vendors covered:** 6/6 (skipped: <list with reasons or "none">)

## Per-Vendor Brief

### 1. Lovable
- **One-liner:** <3 sentences>
- **Pricing (this week):** <tier names + prices>
- **Strengths:** <3 with source links>
- **Weaknesses:** <3>
- **Patterns to copy:** <named items, each with implementation note>
- **Patterns to beat:** <named items, each with our differentiated approach>
- **Verdict:** <import | adapt | recreate | study only | reject> — <one-line reason>
- **Sources:** <link>, <link>, <link>
- **Diff vs <baseline date>:** new_features=<list or "—"> | pricing_changes=<list or "—"> | new_flows=<list or "—"> | removed=<list or "—"> | positioning=<note or "—">

### 2. Bolt.new
… (same shape)

### 3. Cursor (agent mode)
… (same shape)

### 4. Replit Agent
… (same shape)

### 5. Antigravity 2.0
… (same shape)

### 6. Atoms.dev
… (same shape)

## Top 3 Actions to Consider
1. **<short action title>** — frequency=<n>/6 | impact=<1-5> | effort=<S|M|L> | score=<n>
   - Rationale: <one sentence, cite the vendors>
   - Proposed Multica artifact: <skill name | template | prompt patch>
2. **<short action title>** — frequency=<n>/6 | impact=<1-5> | effort=<S|M|L> | score=<n>
   - Rationale: <one sentence>
   - Proposed Multica artifact: <…>
3. **<short action title>** — frequency=<n>/6 | impact=<1-5> | effort=<S|M|L> | score=<n>
   - Rationale: <one sentence>
   - Proposed Multica artifact: <…>

## Top action → routed to Skill Factory
- DEV-NN — <action #1 title>  (sub-issue dispatched this run)

## Next sweep window
<next-monday-09:00-Asia/Kuwait RFC3339>
```

Post via: `multica issue comment add <this-issue-id> --content-stdin` (HEREDOC the body in). Do NOT include any `mention://agent/...` link in this comment.

(6) Auto-dispatch the #1 action as a sub-issue to the Skill Factory Squad. Selection rule: the single highest-scoring action from the Top 3 (the acceptance criterion only requires the top action; only escalate the #2 and #3 if they are tied-on-score and equally well-formed — never invent fillers). The dispatch sub-issue:
   - Title: "BENCHMARK ACTION: <short action title>"
   - Parent: this autopilot's issue id
   - Project: 09 - Research Intelligence (e977793d-b825-448a-a3f1-bb8f59cc1266)
   - Priority: high
   - Status: todo (fires immediately — the action is already scored and rationalized)
   - Assignee: Skill Factory Squad (925a3e48-37f2-4620-870c-b57fe9003981)
   - Description must include: Input (the pattern observed + which vendors ship it), Expected Output (concrete artifact: new skill / template / prompt patch), Acceptance Criteria, Dependencies, Risk, Definition of Done.
   The squad leader fans out internally. Do NOT @mention any agent in the sub-issue body — assignment alone is the trigger.

(7) Pin metadata on this issue ONLY when materially useful for future runs. Recommended keys:
   - `sweep_window_end` (RFC3339)
   - `baseline_brief_id` (DEV-NN of the prior sweep used for diffing)
   - `vendors_covered` (number, expect 6)
   - `vendors_skipped` (string, comma-separated, only if non-empty)
   - `dispatched_count` (number, expect 1; rarely 2–3 if ties)
   - `material_change_vendors` (string, comma-separated list of vendors whose diff was non-empty)
   Use `multica issue metadata set` once each. Skip any key whose value matches what `metadata list` already shows.

(8) Flip this issue's status to `in_review` (`multica issue status <this-issue-id> in_review`) so the CEO Command Squad can verify the week-over-week delta. Do NOT close it — CEO verifies and closes. The dispatched sub-issue stays `todo` for Skill Factory.

(9) Output to stdout (one line only, no comment): `Benchmark Sweep @ <RFC3339> | vendors=<covered>/6 | material_changes=<n> | top3=<n> | dispatched=<n>`.

OPERATING RULES:
- Never block on a single CLI/HTTP failure. Log "skipped <vendor|source>: <reason>" and continue.
- Cap dispatch at 1 sub-issue (acceptance criterion: "Auto-routes top action to Skill Factory Squad"). Only escalate to 2–3 when scores tie and both items are PR-ready specs.
- Read metadata before pinning. Only update keys whose values changed.
- All comments go on the auto-created issue or on the dispatch sub-issue this run spawned. Never comment on DEV-36, DEV-50, DEV-60, or any other unrelated issue.

    - new_blockers: issues that flipped to status=blocked in the window (updated_at >= since AND status=blocked).
    - cleared_blockers: issues that left blocked in the window (status != blocked AND prior blocked_reason metadata key present — best-effort).
    - new_approval_asks: any issue whose metadata gained `waiting_on=owner` or `waiting_on=admin` in the window, or whose body/comments contain a fresh "Approval needed" line written in the window (cheap heuristic: latest comment created_at >= since and contains "approval needed" case-insensitive).
    - stale_in_review_48h: any issue status=in_review with updated_at older than 48h.
    - lifecycle_phase changes: any parent issue whose metadata `lifecycle_phase` changed (compare to value from yesterday's pin — see step 7).
    - new_app_requests: any issue created_at >= since in project `01 - App Requests` (f3089a97-c608-4599-913c-f331335e1abe).

    - lifecycle_phase: read from the project's "anchor" parent issue (heuristic: the parent issue with the most children in this project; if APP project, the parent whose title starts with "APP — "). Show the value of metadata `lifecycle_phase` or "—" if unset.
    - Active issues: counts by status — `todo=N in_progress=N in_review=N blocked=N` (exclude DEV-36).

issues.description:
## BENCHMARK ACTION: Pre-build Research Gate skill

**Dispatched from:** DEV-180 Weekly Benchmark Sweep 2026-06-01
**Score:** 16.0 (frequency=4/6 | impact=4 | effort=S)

---

## Input

**Pattern observed:** 4 of 6 competitors now ship some form of "review or research before code runs":
- **Lovable** — Plan Mode (Feb 2026): shows approach for user approval before any code runs
- **Replit Agent** — plan-while-build (Agent 4): shows execution plan concurrently as build proceeds
- **Atoms.dev** — Iris Deep Research: market research, competitive analysis, and audience sizing before any code runs
- **Cursor** — Auto-review Run Mode v3.6 (May 29): classifier subagent triages Shell/MCP/Fetch calls before execution

**Our current state:** Intake stage exists but has no automated research or pre-build review step. Build agents start immediately after issue creation.

**The gap:** We miss the planning-before-execution pattern that 4/6 competitors ship. This leads to: builds starting without confirming assumptions, rework when requirements are misunderstood, no competitive/market context before the first line of code.

---

## Expected Output

A new skill `pre-build-research-gate` that:
1. Triggers at the start of any build sub-issue (before the build agent runs)
2. Spawns a lightweight research agent that checks: (a) what similar apps exist, (b) what differentiates this one from them, (c) what the riskiest assumption in the issue brief is
3. Outputs a 3-bullet "pre-build brief" as a comment on the build issue
4. Optionally gates code generation: either requires explicit CEO/owner approval OR auto-approves after 60 seconds (configurable per workspace)
5. Stores the brief in issue metadata as `pre_build_brief` for future runs to reference

The skill must be usable as a standalone invocation and as a pre-hook in the standard Build Squad workflow.

---

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Skill file created in project 08 - Skill Library with: purpose, trigger, required input, steps, output, quality checklist, failure handling, improvement rule
- [ ] Skill runs in <3 minutes end-to-end (research agent + comment post)
- [ ] Output is a comment with exactly 3 bullets: (1) comparable apps found, (2) differentiation claim, (3) riskiest assumption
- [ ] Skill works on any build issue with a description ≥ 50 words
- [ ] Configurable gate: `gate_mode: approve | auto_approve_after_60s | inform_only`
- [ ] Failure handling: if research agent returns empty/inconclusive, skill posts "insufficient context — proceeding" and does not block the build
- [ ] Tested on at least one live build issue before marking done

## Dependencies
- 08 - Skill Library project access
- Research-scout agent (606dde5b-1e4b-481a-8cf4-0f6aa0dd37c7) available for consultation on research agent design

## Risk
- **Medium:** If the gate mode is set to `approve`, builds will stall waiting for human approval — default should be `inform_only` until the pattern is validated.
- **Low:** Research agent may produce generic output for narrowly-scoped issues — quality checklist should include a minimum differentiation bar.

## Definition of Done
- Skill file published in 08 - Skill Library with all required sections
- Tested on one live build issue (result attached as comment)
- CEO Command Squad notified the skill is available
- Skill invocable from any build issue description matching the trigger

The skill's required output shape (per DEV-50) is: vendor name, run date, 3-sentence description, onboarding flow note, pricing as of run date, 3 strengths (with source links), 3 weaknesses, patterns to copy (named), patterns to beat (named, with our differentiated approach), decision verdict (import / adapt / recreate / study only / reject). If the `benchmark-vendor-tracker` skill is not yet published, use this inline fallback rubric and add a one-line note to the final report: "benchmark-vendor-tracker skill not yet published — used inline rubric." Capture every claim with a source URL — landing page, pricing page, changelog, release notes, blog post, or YouTube demo. No hand-wavy claims.

   Source-of-truth checklist per vendor (best effort; skip any that 404 or block):
   - Landing page (current copy + above-the-fold positioning)
   - Pricing page (capture tier names, prices, and any "new in <date>" badges)
   - Changelog / release notes / "What's new" page
   - Public blog or product update feed (last 30 days)
   - YouTube / X / public demo for any flow change announced in the window
   Continue on per-source failure. Cap vendor research at ~10 minutes wall time each.

(3) Diff vs last week. For each vendor, compare this week's brief to the baseline brief (from step 1). Material diff categories:
   - new_features (named, with source link)
   - pricing_changes (tier added/removed, price up/down, free-tier change)
   - new_flows (onboarding, agent UX, deployment surface, integration surface)
   - removed_or_deprecated (anything quietly dropped)
   - positioning_shift (the headline copy changed in a way that signals strategy)
   If a vendor genuinely shipped nothing material, write "No material change since <baseline date>." Do NOT pad — empty diffs are signal, not failure.

(4) Score the cross-vendor pattern set. Aggregate every "pattern to copy" across the six briefs and rank by:
   - frequency (how many of the six vendors do this)
   - impact (1–5: how much would importing this move our win rate vs Lovable/Bolt/Cursor)
   - effort (S | M | L)
   - readiness (do we already have an adjacent skill we can extend, or is this net-new)
   Final score = (frequency × impact) / effort. Pick the Top 3 "actions to consider."

Run the `workspace-upgrade-report` skill against the live workspace and post a single ranked action list as a comment on the issue this autopilot creates. Auto-dispatch the top 3 actions to skill-builder, prompt-optimizer, and memory-curator. Never skip a week — a silent skip is itself a finding.

CRITICAL SAFETY RULES:
- Mode is create_issue. Do not create additional issues except the three sub-issues for top-3 dispatch in step (6).
- NEVER mention CEO in any comment — CEO is the assignee and re-mentioning would loop (Factory Pulse v3 no-mention rule).
- NEVER mention any agent in a wrap-up. Only mention agents to delegate first-touch work on a fresh sub-issue.
- NEVER touch DEV-36 (the workspace-operations dialog).
- Read each issue's metadata before pinning anything; do not log run state. Stay within the issue this autopilot creates.

STEPS:

(1) Scope the scan window. Treat `since = now - 7 days` (RFC3339, UTC). Use `--output json` on every CLI call. Continue on single-call failure (log to stdout and skip that signal).

(2) Collect the five input signals:

   (a) retry_logs (last 7 days). Walk every issue updated in the past 7 days and pull metadata. Any `retry_log` key present is a failure signature. Aggregate by skill name / agent / failure_reason.
       - `multica issue list --output json --status in_progress` and `--status blocked` and `--status in_review` (paged if needed).
       - For each issue, `multica issue metadata list <id> --output json` — keep only entries with a `retry_log` key.

   (b) Blocked issues — every issue currently `status=blocked`:
       - `multica issue list --status blocked --output json`
       - For each: capture id, identifier, title, assignee, age (now - updated_at), and `blocked_reason` metadata.

   (c) Stale in_review (>48h) — every issue currently `status=in_review` whose `updated_at` is more than 48h ago:
       - `multica issue list --status in_review --output json`
       - Filter client-side by `updated_at`.

   (d) Agent WIP imbalance — for every agent in the workspace, count `in_progress` issues assigned to them. Flag any agent with WIP=0 (cold) or WIP≥4 (overloaded). Flag any squad whose total WIP ≥7.
       - `multica agent list --output json`
       - `multica issue list --status in_progress --output json` and group by `assignee_id`.

   (e) Skill orphans — every skill not assigned to any agent (`agent_count==0`), and every agent with zero skills attached.

(3) Score and rank actions. For each candidate upgrade action, score on:
   - Impact (how many issues/agents this would unblock or accelerate; 1–5)

- [ ] Factory Pulse autopilot updated to dispatch Growth before DevOps deploy

## Dependencies
- Issues for `growth-launch-plan` and `landing-page-builder` skills (siblings)

## Risk
Adding a 10th squad widens routing surface. Mitigation: Factory Pulse already handles squad dispatch; only one new branch needed.

## Definition of Done
Squad live, manual published, Factory Pulse routing rule updated, post comment with squad-id on this issue.

## User request

You are the Factory Kernel CEO of this Multica workspace.

Your mission is to turn this workspace into a world-class autonomous AI App Factory that is faster, smarter, and more complete than Lovable, Antigravity 2.0, Atoms.dev, Bolt, Replit Agent, Cursor, and normal Claude Code workflows.

You are not a chatbot. You are the operating brain of an elite AI software company.

MAIN OBJECTIVE:
When I give you any idea, you must turn it into a complete product with maximum speed and quality:
- research
- product strategy
- PRD
- UX/UI
- database
- APIs
- frontend
- backend
- auth
- integrations
- QA
- security
- deployment
- documentation
- SEO/growth
- reusable skills
- continuous improvement

OPERATING MODEL:
Use Multica as the control plane for projects, issues, squads, agents, skills, tracking, and execution.
Use Hermes as the long-running runtime for memory, planning, learning, self-improvement, and skill refinement.
Use Claude Code as the deep coding engine for repo analysis, coding, debugging, testing, refactoring, hooks, subagents, MCP, and plugins.
Use GitHub research to discover useful skills, templates, MCP tools, app-builder patterns, and open-source workflows.
Use Rainbow-style workflow:
regulate → assess-context → specify → design → taskify → implement → validate → improve.

IMMEDIATE MODE:
Enter FAST FACTORY MODE now.

PHASE 1 — FULL WORKSPACE AUDIT
Audit the entire workspace:
- projects
- issues
- squads
- agents
- skills
- runtimes
- autopilots
- templates
- context
- blocked work
- idle work
- weak outputs
- duplicated agents
- duplicated skills
- missing QA/security/deployment systems

Return:
- what works
- what is incomplete
- what is weak
- what is duplicated
- what is missing
- what is risky
- what can run in parallel immediately

PHASE 2 — COMMAND CENTER
Create a live Command Center.

Track every project:
- goal
- phase
- priority
- active issues
- squad
- agents
- status
- blockers
- risks
- next action
- expected output
- quality gate
- approval needed or not

Track every issue:
- title
- owner
- squad
- priority
- input
- output
- dependencies
- acceptance criteria
- definition of done
- current status
- next action

No task can stay vague, idle, unassigned, blocked, or completed without review.

PHASE 3 — PARALLEL EXECUTION ENGINE
For every new app/system idea, split work into these parallel tracks:

1. Product Track — Owner: Product Squad
Output: product brief, market/use-case validation, MVP scope, production scope, PRD, user stories, acceptance criteria, role matrix.

2. UX/UI Track — Owner: UX Squad
Output: sitemap, screen list, user flows, dashboard layout, mobile layout, empty states, loading states, error states, RTL/Arabic rules when needed.

3. Architecture Track — Owner: Architecture Squad
Output: recommended stack, database schema, ERD, API contracts, auth model, permissions, integrations, security model.

4. Build Track — Owner: Build Squad
Output: frontend, backend, database setup, auth, APIs, integrations, tests, code review.

5. QA/Security Track — Owner: QA Security Squad
Output: test plan, test cases, bug reports, permission review, security review, performance review.

6. DevOps/Docs Track — Owner: Launch Squad
Output: environment checklist, deployment plan, rollback plan, release notes, admin guide, user guide, handover checklist.

7. Growth Track — Owner: Growth Squad
Output: landing page plan, SEO plan, onboarding flow, analytics events, conversion checklist, launch campaign plan.

8. Skill Factory Track — Owner: Skill Factory Squad
Output: reusable skills, improved prompts, improved issue templates, improved agent instructions, post-build learning report.

Execution rules:
- Start Product, UX, and Architecture together.
- Start QA planning before coding is complete.
- Start DevOps planning before final QA.
- Start Growth planning before launch.
- Start Build once architecture is clear enough.
- Start Skill Factory as soon as a workflow repeats.
- Never wait if another safe parallel track can move.

PHASE 4 — GITHUB RESEARCH ENGINE
Continuously research and evaluate:
- Multica latest docs/changelog/issues
- Hermes Agent latest docs/releases
- Claude Code skills/hooks/subagents/MCP/plugins
- Anthropic official skills
- Supabase agent skills
- awesome-agent-skills
- awesome-claude-code-toolkit
- awesome-claude-code-subagents
- hanoi-rainbow
- open-source AI app builders
- open-source agent orchestrators
- Next.js/Supabase/TypeScript templates
- MCP servers useful for app building
- Lovable, Antigravity, Atoms.dev, Bolt, Replit, Cursor as benchmarks

For every source:
- summarize what it does
- identify useful patterns
- identify useful skills/templates
- evaluate compatibility
- identify risks
- decide: import, adapt, recreate, study only, or reject

Do not blindly install anything. Do not trust community skills until reviewed.

PHASE 5 — SKILL FACTORY
Create and improve reusable skills.

Core skills to create/improve now:
- idea-to-product-brief
- product-brief-to-prd
- prd-to-user-stories
- user-stories-to-issues
- ux-screen-generator
- database-schema-designer
- api-contract-designer
- auth-permission-matrix
- nextjs-supabase-builder
- crm-system-builder
- hr-system-builder
- delivery-system-builder
- dashboard-builder
- admin-panel-builder
- qa-test-plan
- security-review
- deployment-checklist
- growth-launch-plan
- post-build-learning-report
- agent-prompt-improver
- workspace-upgrade-loop

Every skill must include: name, purpose, trigger, inputs, steps, tools, output format, quality checklist, failure handling, security notes, improvement rule.

After every task ask:
- can this become a reusable skill?
- can this existing skill be improved?
- can this workflow become a template?
- did any agent need better instructions?
- can the next run be faster?

PHASE 6 — BENCHMARK ENGINE
Benchmark this workspace against: Lovable, Antigravity 2.0, Atoms.dev, Bolt, Replit Agent, Cursor, Claude Code-only workflows.

For each benchmark, extract:
- what they do better
- what we can copy as a workflow
- what we can beat
- what skill/template/agent we need
- what should be added to the App Factory

Goal: Do not copy them blindly. Build a stronger system using Multica squads, Hermes memory, Claude Code execution, GitHub skills, QA gates, Growth engine, and self-improvement.

PHASE 7 — QUALITY GATES
No app/system is complete unless it has: clear product goal, PRD, user stories, screen list, database schema, API plan, auth/roles, permissions, responsive UI, validation, loading states, empty states, error states, QA checklist, security review, performance review, deployment plan, rollback plan, documentation, SEO/growth plan, reusable skill extraction.

PHASE 8 — SAFETY RULES
Never: expose secrets; edit credentials without approval; delete data without approval; deploy to production without approval; install unknown tools without evaluation; run destructive commands without approval; import external skills without security review; mark work complete without quality gates; create duplicate agents or skills without reason.

PHASE 9 — RECURRING AUTOPILOTS
Create or propose recurring operations:

Daily: Command Center review, blocked issue review, idle agent review, completed output review, top priority execution plan.
Weekly: GitHub research update, benchmark report, skill library cleanup, agent performance review, workspace upgrade plan, security review.
Monthly: architecture review, tool stack review, App Factory playbook update, roadmap refresh.

If you cannot create autopilots directly, give exact manual setup steps.

START NOW:
1. Re-audit the workspace.
2. Build the Command Center.
3. Identify critical gaps.
4. Create the first 20 issues.
5. Assign issues to squads and agents.
6. Launch safe parallel work.
7. Create or improve the first 20 skills.
8. Start GitHub research tasks.
9. Start benchmark research.
10. Create the 24-hour execution plan.
11. Create the 7-day upgrade plan.
12. Tell me exactly what needs my approval.

FINAL OUTPUT FORMAT:
Return only:
1. Workspace Status
2. Critical Gaps
3. Command Center
4. Parallel Execution Plan
5. First 20 Issues To Launch
6. First 20 Skills To Create or Improve
7. GitHub Research Tasks
8. Benchmark Plan
9. Quality Gates
10. Autopilots To Create
11. 24-Hour Execution Plan
12. 7-Day Upgrade Plan
13. What Requires User Approval
14. Exact Next Action

STYLE:
Be fast. Be specific. Be practical. No long theory. No fake completion. No vague advice. Always assign owners. Always create next actions. Always improve the machine.

issues.description:
# ADR-002 — App Hub Model (per-app project + per-app repo + codegraph brain + local-first lifecycle)

**Status:** Adopted 2026-05-25.
**Supersedes:** Default routing where every Build child lands in `05 - Parallel Build`.
**Driver:** [DEV-36](mention://issue/e60d5be5-b9dd-48aa-b949-28286ea80f1b) — workspace owner asked for a coherent per-project structure, local-first integration, single integrated deploy at the end, and a code-graph layer in the style of [Understand-Anything](https://github.com/Lum1104/Understand-Anything) and [codegraph](https://github.com/colbymchenry/codegraph).

---

## 1. The change in one line
**Every app/system gets its own Multica project + its own GitHub repo + its own codegraph index. Phase projects (00–09) become the workspace's *library*, not the place where work lives.**

---

## 2. New project taxonomy

| Layer | Purpose | Examples |
|-------|---------|----------|
| **Library projects** (`00 - Factory Brain` … `09 - Research Intelligence`) | Standards, ADRs, skills, templates, research. Long-lived. Cross-app. | ADRs, the Skill Library, intake forms. |
| **App Hub projects** (`APP — <name>`) | One per app/system. **All execution issues for that app live here** — PRD, schema, build, QA, deploy, docs. Closes when the app is shipped. | `APP — Android Attendance`, `APP — HR Dashboard`, … |

The current scattered routing (PRD in `02`, schema in `04`, build children in `05`, QA in `06`, deploy in `07`) is replaced with: **every artifact for app X lives in `APP — X` and carries a `phase=<intake|spec|design|arch|build|integrate|qa|deploy|done>` label.** Cross-app skill/standard updates still live in the library projects.

## 3. Per-app GitHub repo

- Convention: `newtechkw/<app-slug>` (e.g. `newtechkw/attendance-app`).
- Created **once** at the Architecture Gate by `deployment-engineer` (escalation if no PAT).
- Attached to the app hub via `multica project resource add <hub-project-id> --type github_repo --url <repo-url>`.
- Default branch `main`. Two protected branches: `main` (deploy target) and `integrate` (continuous integration target).
- Pinned on the app's parent issue as `app_repo_url`.

## 4. Local-first lifecycle (the hard rule)

Build engineers run **everything locally** until the parent is in `in_review` with QA + Security green. No `vercel deploy`, no `eas build --profile production`, no `npm publish`, no `supabase db push --linked`. Preview deploys (`vercel --prod=false`, `eas build --profile preview`, local Supabase via Docker) are allowed and encouraged for integration testing.

Phases (mirrored in the parent issue metadata `lifecycle_phase`):

```
intake → spec → design → arch → build → integrate → qa → deploy → done
```

- `build` ends only when every build sub-issue is `done` AND the codegraph reports no orphan endpoints / missing handlers.
- `integrate` = one combined PR (or one weekly integration PR) merging every build stream into `integrate` branch + green local E2E run.
- `qa` = QA Security Squad veto gate on the `integrate` branch, not on a deployed environment.
- `deploy` = **single integrated deploy**, owned by DevOps Launch Squad, executes only after QA + Security sign-off on `integrate`.

## 5. Codegraph layer (the shared brain)

Inspired by [codegraph](https://github.com/colbymchenry/codegraph) (SQLite + tree-sitter + MCP) and [Understand-Anything](https://github.com/Lum1104/Understand-Anything) (multi-agent indexer + JSON knowledge graph).

Per repo:
- `.codegraph/codegraph.db` (SQLite, tree-sitter symbol/edge graph, FTS5).
- `.understand/graph.json` (architectural overview + business-domain map).
- Refreshed by the `codegraph-indexer` agent on every PR merge to `integrate` (and on demand).
- Exposed to other agents via MCP (`codegraph_search`, `codegraph_trace`, `codegraph_callers`, `codegraph_impact`) — wires into Build + QA agents so they reason on indexed symbols instead of grepping. Net effect from the upstream project: ~35% fewer tokens, ~71% fewer tool calls.

Owner: **Knowledge Graph Squad** (new — see §7).

## 6. Per-app metadata contract

Pinned on the **app's parent issue** (e.g. DEV-25):

| Key | Example | Why |
|-----|---------|-----|
| `app_project_id` | `<hub-uuid>` | Pointer to the per-app Multica project. |
| `app_repo_url` | `https://github.com/newtechkw/attendance-app` | Single source of truth for the codebase. |
| `lifecycle_phase` | `build` | Drives Factory Pulse routing. |
| `local_run_command` | `pnpm dev` or `expo start` | What a fresh agent runs to verify locally. |
| `integration_branch` | `integrate` | QA target before deploy. |
| `codegraph_status` | `fresh@2026-05-25T18:00Z` | Last indexer run timestamp. |
| `deploy_gate` | `blocked: qa-pending` / `green` | Hard gate read by `deployment-engineer`. |
| `waiting_on` | (existing) | External blockers (credentials, paid resources). |

## 7. New squad: Knowledge Graph Squad

Members:
- **codegraph-indexer** (leader) — runs codegraph + understand-anything pipelines on PR merge, refreshes `.codegraph/codegraph.db` and `.understand/graph.json`, posts a diff-impact summary on the merge issue.
- **codebase-cartographer** — owns the human-readable architecture overview (`docs/ARCHITECTURE.md`), the guided tours, the symbol naming conventions, and the cross-repo symbol vocabulary.
- **impact-analyst** — on every Build sub-issue, runs `codegraph_impact` against the proposed diff scope and posts the affected-symbols list so reviewers know the blast radius.

Trigger: PR opened or merged on any `APP — *` repo. Skill: `codegraph-index-refresh` (to be authored).

## 8. New skill: `app-hub-bootstrap`

Steps:
1. Create `APP — <name>` Multica project (icon, description, lead = ceo).
2. Provision GitHub repo `newtechkw/<slug>` from template (template repo to be set up: includes Next.js + Supabase + Expo skeleton + codegraph install script + Understand-Anything CLI). **Requires GH PAT.**
3. Attach repo to project via `multica project resource add`.
4. Seed `main` and `integrate` branches.
5. Run `codegraph init` + `understand-anything init` on the empty repo so future merges have a baseline.
6. Pin the metadata contract on the parent app issue.
7. Hand to Product UX Squad for PRD.

## 9. Factory Pulse v3 (incremental, not destructive)

Add to existing autopilot **without changing current exclusions**:
- Read every issue's `app_project_id` metadata; route dispatch by `lifecycle_phase` first, falling back to current rules.
- New rule: when parent `lifecycle_phase=build` and every build child is `done`, flip to `integrate`, comment "Build complete — opening integration PR" and reassign the parent to Knowledge Graph Squad to run impact analysis + open the integration PR.
- New rule: never reassign the parent to DevOps Launch Squad until both `deploy_gate=green` AND `lifecycle_phase=qa→deploy`.
- DEV-36 + member-conversation exclusions remain.

## 10. What requires admin (credentials)

These are the only blockers — every other piece of this ADR is executable today:

1. **GitHub PAT** with `repo:create` scope on `newtechkw` org (so `app-hub-bootstrap` can create repos unattended).
2. **Codegraph + Understand-Anything install on the runtime host** — both are MIT-licensed npm/Python packages; the install is reversible but it modifies the runtime image.
3. Re-confirmation that **`newtechkw` is the canonical GitHub org**, not a personal account.

## 11. Migration plan for the apps already in flight

- **DEV-25 (Android Attendance)** — create `APP — Android Attendance` hub now, link via metadata, leave the in-flight children (DEV-26…DEV-35) where they are to avoid disrupting Factory Pulse v2 mid-build. Future children attach to the hub.
- Next inbound app uses the full hub model from intake.

## 12. Rejected alternatives

- **One mega-repo for all apps.** Rejected: codegraph index becomes 10× larger, cross-app blast radius hides real impact, deploy of one app forces a re-deploy of everything.
- **Keep using phase projects for execution + attach repo at workspace level.** Rejected: today's pain (no per-app container, scattered children, no per-app codegraph) doesn't go away.
- **Build the codegraph layer in-house.** Rejected: both upstream projects are MIT, mature enough, and battle-tested with agentic users — adopting beats rebuilding.

---

## Definition of Done for this ADR
- `APP — Android Attendance` project exists and DEV-25 has the metadata contract pinned. ✅ (executed in this run.)
- Knowledge Graph Squad + codegraph-indexer agent exist. ✅
- `app-hub-bootstrap` skill issue exists in `08 - Skill Library`. ✅
- Factory Pulse v3 patch drafted (deployed in a follow-up after user nod). ⏳
- GitHub repo provisioning escalation logged on DEV-25. ⏳ (requires admin.)
